title: Comparing Things

contentBlocks.text: Python has six comparison operators: == equal, != not equal, > greater than, < less than, >= greater or equal, <= less or equal.
There are two variables, coins and price. Create a variable named can_afford that is True if coins is greater than or equal to price.

starterCode:
coins = 40
price = 25

# Set can_afford based on whether coins >= price
solutionCode:
coins = 40
price = 25
can_afford = coins >= price
print(can_afford)

testCases.failureMessage: can_afford should be coins >= price, which is True here.

hints: Use >= to compare: can_afford = coins >= price
Print it so you can check your answer: print(can_afford)
